commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
Adds a provider-stream guard and focused tests to reject a `google` provider config whose API is explicitly non-Google while preserving env-only direct Gemini routing.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +127. Total +161 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows a current-main `models.providers.google.api: "google-vertex"` config resolves to `provider: "google"` and `api: "google-vertex"`, while the PR guard throws for any configured Google API other than `google-generative-ai`.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can adjust the guard and tests to preserve Google Vertex while retaining the explicit non-Google mismatch failure.

target.title: fix: fail closed for missing Google provider config
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85042.

This narrows Google provider/model validation to the unsafe explicit mismatch case while preserving documented env-only Gemini auth.

A `google/gemini-*` model is still allowed when `models.providers.google` is absent, so `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `GOOGLE_API_KEY` setups keep working. If `models.providers.google` is explicitly present but points at a non-Google API such as `openai-responses`, OpenClaw fails closed before stream dispatch.

## Security impact

This prevents a Google model selection from being routed through a mismatched OpenAI-compatible provider config while preserving the existing env-backed Google provider contract.

The error message does not include configured API keys.

## Validation

Focused tests cover both the unsafe mismatch and the preserved env-only Google path:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /home/rev/projects/openclaw-pr-85042-clean

✓ agents-core ../../src/agents/provider-stream.test.ts (4 tests) 40ms
✓ agents-core ../../src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/stream-resolution.test.ts (20 tests) 9ms
✓ agents-pi-embedded ../../src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/stream-resolution.test.ts (20 tests) 20ms
✓ agents-support ../../src/agents/provider-stream.test.ts (4 tests) 41ms

Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests       48 passed (48)
Duration    4.02s
```

Build validation:

```text
build exit=0
CLI bootstrap import guard passed.
OK: All 4 required plugin-sdk exports verified.
```

## Real behavior proof

behavior:
Run the OpenClaw CLI local embedded agent path with `--model google/gemini-3.5-flash` while `models.providers.google` is explicitly misconfigured as `api: "openai-responses"`.

environment:
Local OpenClaw source checkout from this PR branch with isolated runtime state.

```text
repo: /home/rev/projects/openclaw-pr-85042-clean
head: 85dd459add
node: v26.0.0
pnpm: 11.2.2
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH: /tmp/openclaw-85042-proof.QGKoBm/openclaw.json
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR: /tmp/openclaw-85042-proof.QGKoBm/state
```

steps:
1. Created a temporary OpenClaw config.
2. Configured `agents.defaults.model.primary` as `google/gemini-3.5-flash`.
3. Allowed `google/gemini-3.5-flash` in `agents.defaults.models`.
4. Configured `models.providers.google.api` as `openai-responses`.
5. Set `models.providers.google.apiKey` to a sentinel secret, then redacted it in the proof.
6. Ran `node ./openclaw.mjs agent --local --session-key proof-85042 --message proof --model google/gemini-3.5-flash --json`.
7. Confirmed OpenClaw failed locally with the provider/API mismatch error before OpenAI-compatible dispatch.

evidence:

Config excerpt used for the proof:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "model": {
        "primary": "google/gemini-3.5-flash"
      },
      "models": {
        "google/gemini-3.5-flash": {}
      }
    }
  },
  "models": {
    "providers": {
      "google": {
        "api": "openai-responses",
        "apiKey": "[REDACTED]"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Command:

```bash
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-85042-proof.QGKoBm/openclaw.json \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-85042-proof.QGKoBm/state \
node ./openclaw.mjs agent \
  --local \
  --session-key proof-85042 \
  --message proof \
  --model google/gemini-3.5-flash \
  --json
```

Redacted output excerpt:

```text
[diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=12387 error="Error: Google model "google/gemini-3.5-flash" cannot use models.providers.google api "openai-responses". Expected api "google-generative-ai". Configure the Google provider correctly or remove the mismatched provider config."
[diagnostic] lane task error: lane=session:agent:main:proof-85042 durationMs=12391 error="Error: Google model "google/gemini-3.5-flash" cannot use models.providers.google api "openai-responses". Expected api "google-generative-ai". Configure the Google provider correctly or remove the mismatched provider config."
Error: Google model "google/gemini-3.5-flash" cannot use models.providers.google api "openai-responses". Expected api "google-generative-ai". Configure the Google provider correctly or remove the mismatched provider config.

exitCode: 1
containsMismatchMessage: yes
containsExpectedGoogleApi: yes
reachedOpenAITransport: no
secretLeaked: no
```

observedResult:
OpenClaw fails locally through the CLI embedded agent path with a clear provider/API mismatch error before dispatch. The proof confirms `reachedOpenAITransport: no`, so the run did not reach the OpenAI-compatible transport. The proof also confirms `secretLeaked: no`.

notTested:
A live request to Google Generative AI was not performed.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.reason: author association CONTRIBUTOR and repository permission none are not allowed
